All right, you're back. I want to focus now on the tonal aspects and communication aspects, in terms of the words that you would say, to make sure that when you're done with your presentation, meaning you close the sale, that when you transition to this next part, the after sale, everything goes as planned. All right? So it starts with understanding that once you close, your job then shifts to make sure that the certainty that you've built up during the sale continues to build over time. So the prospect that now you're a new customer, essentially, they were a prospect, right? They continue to feel like they've made an awesome decision. You get it? Now, to that end, there are three things that you can do right away to help essentially wow your customer and create a top notch customer experience that will begin the process of buying them to you for the long term. Let me go through them one by one. Number one, the first thing you need to realize here is that when someone says, yes, that's just the beginning here. It's not the end of the sales process. See, the biggest mistake that most salespeople make is that the moment they close the sale, they let their guard down. They almost drop their level of certainty. Essentially they lowered their own energy level. I think they already got things sewed up. In reality, what you need to do is the exact opposite of that. In other words, your goal is that when you close the sale, you want to immediately anchor into your customer this feeling they've made this great decision. You want to give them that extra dose of certainty that everything's going to continue to work out exactly as they envisioned it over the long term. The way you do this is the moment when you actually get that yes, I almost want you to trigger yourself into even a higher state of certainty. In other words, you want you to raise your energy level after the customer says yes versus lowering it. Now, that being said, what I'm not talking about here is you're going, yeah, welcome aboard Mr. Jones. You made a great decision. I don't mean that. What I'm talking about here is that bottled enthusiasm, that bottled energy that you have, that the customer understands you, that you're excited, they made a great decision, and you're still that same person that you were before they said yes. What I see a lot of salespeople do is they actually close the sale and they're like, all right, great, Mr. Jones, thanks a lot, great, welcome, bye. They think the sale's over. That's not it. What you want to have is an anchored language pattern here of exactly what you're going to say to someone after they say, I say, sir, you made an excellent decision. Number one, I want to welcome you aboard. Let me give you my personal number here, my cell phone number, if that's what you're going to do, right? And here's my exact email address to get in touch with you. You can text me as well, my WhatsApp number. And again, sir, any problems that happen, we obviously don't foresee anything, any issues, any challenges arise, please get on the phone. Immediately, I'm here for you as well as the customer success department. Now, if you're going to actually do a handoff to customer success department, right? Guess what? You want to actually arrange that handoff yourself right then and there on the phone. But make sure that the customer knows that they're going to still be dealing with you if that's the case. In other words, in some companies, it's not going to be the case. Don't say that. But if that is going to be the case, but you're still going to introduce another person and that's going to help with the onboarding, let them know what's going to happen. Let me introduce you to so-and-so, who's our customer success expert. They're going to make sure you get on boarded correctly, answer any of your questions. But of course, sir, also I will be there. Here's my personal information, any issues at all, we don't foresee them, but any issues at all, I'll be there for you 24, 7, 365. Believe me, sir, you made a great decision. Welcome aboard. Now, just so you know, I just made that up. I don't have a script in free. I made that up. Now, obviously, you notice I missed a couple of words there. Why? Because if I was going to be doing this real, I would never just wing it. I'd have the perfect pattern written out for me. So this is crucial that when the sale is closed, they say, yep, great, all right, fine. You'll say, okay, great, sir, thanks a lot. Very welcome aboard. Bye. That's not going to end well. I promise you. Anchor them into, they made a great decision. You have your anchor language pattern, okay, which we're going to actually construct in the exercise portion of this lesson here. I'm going to show you exactly how to do it, give you a guide to start, right? Make sure you anchor them in, do a seamless handoff to anybody else who will be involved in the process. You give them whatever information you're supposed to give them and bam, just like that, you've set yourself up for greatness. Now, in terms of how we start to engage in upsells, that's a separate lesson. What I want you to know right now is that unless you commence with a perfect handoff, your upsell ratio is going to do a nosedive. That's number one. Now, number two, I want you to follow up your sale whenever possible. Meaning it's not going to apply to everybody, but whenever possible with a personal thank you note, let me just tell you this. Writing thank you notes is pretty much a lost art and because of that, handwritten notes, especially making even bigger impact than they ever have in the past. In other words, people don't really get them anymore, right? So when you actually do get one, you're pretty much blown away. Let me be an example here. A few years back, I was out for lunch with one of my book agents. My name is Joe Gottler, great guy, good friend of mine, right? We were talking about a famous ex Hollywood agent named Michael Owitz, named Mike Ringelbelter. He was the first super agent in Hollywood, the founder of CAA, right? And here's the deal. He was not the sort of guy that had a very good reputation. He's a controversial hard edge character. So I always wondered, what was it about this guy that made him so powerful? How did he build this business? So I said to Joe, what was it about Mike Owitz? How did he go about getting every major star to sign up and what was his secret? And here's what Joel said to me. He goes, this guy was the world's greatest ingratiator. You know what I mean, ingratiators? It's someone that makes you kind of like them and almost feel beholden to them. Here's what he did. This guy had four assistants working around the clock, scouring through the trades, and any time he saw that someone got any type of award or received an accolade or a nomination, he would send them a small congratulatory and written note or a gift. He writes his little heartwarming notes as if he actually cared in spite of the fact that his reputation would say he did anything but care. You know, art of stone, right? But because of these wonderful and gratiating congratulatory notes, and by sending out relentless again and again and again, people would go and say, well, maybe he does care about me despite the fact they probably knew he didn't and it worked like a charm. See here's the deal. Now, in this case, writing ingratiating notes like this as this is a lead jet, separate issues. So I'm not totally cost to recite that. I do cross over. I'm giving you both sides here. And he was doing it to actually his lead generation. And you can do that as well. It's just a very expensive and cumbersome proposition. And you have to make sure you're going at the very high value targets. So you can also use this for people that you're trying to get through to when you notice they've done things that are press worthy or anything that really is a birthday or something you can get through to someone with a reason. You write this congratulatory note. It goes a really long way to ingratiating someone else towards you. Now, on the other hand, why it's so relevant to this situation is that after you close someone and now they actually are a client, wow, you can really lock this person in by sending them a simple three sentence note. Okay. Right after they've received your product or service, it's all to them, whatever it might be. And remember, it has to be very lean. Not like it's not like a massive, you know, long note and it's in the direction of a heart warming emotional few sentences. If you do this right, the impact it has on the person is staggering. And by the way, also the monitor, yes, it can be done through email as well, text, something needs to go out. I love the handwritten notes because for the high ticket sales boy, it goes a really long way. And again, for Mike, oh, this was a lead gen angle. Okay. You can also use it with tremendous effectiveness when you use it as a post closing mechanism. You get it? Now, one very important point here. Remember, you have this dichotomy here of, well, okay, when I was gathering intelligence, I wasn't supposed to go off the Pluto and find out about things that are not germane to the sale. Children's birthdays when they're winning anniversaries, what's their favorite hobby? Sometimes it comes up in passing, but often not. Well, guess what? Now that the sale is closed, the rules of what defines going off the Pluto are completely changed. Now you'll want to actually go off the Pluto. There's no longer Pluto. You want to find about what this person's life's about outside of work. What hobbies do they have? What things do they enjoy? Where do they like to go to sporting events? What sports do they like? What music events they like? What rock groups? What concerts do they like to ballet? You want to know everything about this person because one of the things we're going to discuss in the next lesson, or maybe the lesson after that, is how do you give ethical gifts the right way? There's a way to give ethical gifts that is just so ridiculously effective when it comes to getting up sales and building customers for life that it will just blow you away. Okay? So now the rules do change. So what we want to do here, this is your next step in the process, is that once you close, if you have time at a cause for it on this initial conversation, you want to get into a post-closing conversation that starts to identify what their needs are outside of just the product that you're selling. Powerful stuff. You ask them questions about their family. What hobbies they like. Let it come up naturally. You ask follow-up questions. Same things that you did before when you were gathering intelligence, before you closed, do the same thing, revise qualification session after you closed. Now in many cases, the time is short, it doesn't call for it. So what you're going to do here as part of your upsell process, and we'll get to this in more detail in the next lesson or so, is that I'm going to want you to engage in what we call schmooze calls. These are, and there's no negative connotation there. You know what kind of sounds like there is. A schmooze call is simply when you call up a new client just to say, hey, how are things going to update you on what's going on with your situation, whether it's a stock or a bond or a service or a new product that's being shipped or maybe they already have it. Ask them how it is, how it is going, ask any questions, not looking to sell them a single thing. That's the deal. You call just to check in, update them, bond. And also, if you weren't able to find out the things that you need to find out in terms of what makes them feel alive outside of work, now is the time to dive into this revised qualification session and find out the things you need to know. You get it? Very, very powerful. And we'll go a long way now to setting up what comes next, which means the ability to essentially do upsells, crossells, and get referrals by focusing on ingratiating yourself to this new customer and also solving any other needs they might have along the way as you start to qualify them. You know on some level what this is about is, remember I said, you know, you walk into the office and you see the fish on the wall, don't talk about the fish. Everyone else talk about the fish. Guess what? Now it's time to talk about the fish. You want to find out the things that make this new client feel alive outside of work and things about their family, their children, you know, other kids going to college. Here's a perfect example of how you use that intelligence now to write a great thank you note. Let's say you were a financial planner. You just closed a client. Well, had a daughter who's going to college right in the near future. You'd write, you know, dear John, thank you so much for entrusting with your business. I look forward to helping you build your daughter Emily's college fund, something like that. You get that's a heartwarming note where you talk about the children. Emily goes a long way towards establishing that bond. It's just an example and you can actually personalize it even further after that using things that you uncovered during this revised intelligence gallery phase. You get it? So let's review really quickly here. Number one, when you close the sale key, you don't drop your state. You actually elevate your state, anchor that prospect into a good feeling that he made a great decision, not outward energy, but inward energy was very positive. Actually no difference than before, right? Number two, you want to send that effective thank you note, a powerful thank you note that tugs on the emotional hostage and I will get into that in much more detail in the next lesson, the lesson after. And number three, make sure that you now engage in that revised qualification session to make sure that you know the personal things you need to know about this person and also allow them to know with that post closing conversation that you're there for them. And we're going to create this pattern by the way in the first exercise here that you're there, that you're going to use them for the short term and the low term, any problems that they have. You're there on the spot. We do a seamless handoff and boom, you've set yourself up for greatness, which will be your next shmoos call. And then after that, you're going to start the process of upselling and cross selling, bundling, joint ventures and everything else. All right, so let's stop now. Let's take our quiz, make sure you go through some important stuff. We're going to write out some important patterns here and I'll see you in the next lesson.
